contents.value: **proc** — Tcl keyword.
**set** — Tcl keyword.
**if** — Tcl keyword.
**elseif** — Tcl keyword.
**else** — Tcl keyword.
**puts** — Built-in function/command in Tcl.
**gets** — Built-in function/command in Tcl.
**read** — Built-in function/command in Tcl.
**open** — Built-in function/command in Tcl.
**close** — Built-in function/command in Tcl.
A **function** is a reusable block of code that performs a specific task.
A **variable** stores a value that can be referenced and modified.
A **loop** repeats a block of code while a condition is true or for each item in a collection.
A **conditional** executes different code branches based on a boolean test.
A **string** is a sequence of characters used for text.
An **array** (or list) is an ordered collection of elements.
A **dictionary** (hash/map) stores key-value pairs for fast lookup.
A **class** defines a blueprint for creating objects with attributes and methods.
A **module** is a file containing reusable code that can be imported.
An **exception** signals an error condition that can be caught and handled.
A **lambda** is a concise anonymous function expression.
A **closure** captures variables from its enclosing scope.
An **iterator** yields values one at a time from a sequence.
A **generator** lazily produces values using yield.
A **decorator** wraps a function to extend its behavior.

```tcl
list [arg1 arg2 ...]
```
Creates a properly-formed Tcl list from the given arguments with correct quoting and escaping, serving as the primary list constructor for building data structures.

```tcl
file subcommand [args ...]
```
Ensemble command for filesystem operations including path manipulation, file attribute queries, existence testing, copying, renaming, and deleting files and directories.

```tcl
regexp [-nocase] [-expanded] [-line] [--] exp string [matchVar subMatch1 ...]
```
Tests if a string matches a regular expression pattern and optionally captures matched subexpressions into variables, returning 1 on match and 0 on no match.

```tcl
namespace subcommand [args ...]
```
Manages Tcl namespaces for organizing commands and variables into hierarchical scopes, preventing name collisions and enabling modular package design with import and export control.

```tcl
package subcommand [args ...]
```
Manages Tcl packages for code distribution and version control, supporting require for loading dependencies and provide for declaring package availability with semantic versioning.

```tcl
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```tcl
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```tcl
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```tcl
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```tcl
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```tcl
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```tcl
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```tcl
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```tcl
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
